steps.title: Match the requested root form
steps.content:
The problem gives a solution form involving $\sqrt{k}$, so solve the quadratic far enough to identify the number under the radical. For $3x^2 - 12x - 5 = 0$, identify $a = 3$, $b = -12$, and $c = -5$.

Calculate the discriminant:
$$\Delta = b^2 - 4ac = (-12)^2 - 4(3)(-5) = 144 + 60 = 204$$
Apply the quadratic formula:
$$x = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a} = \frac{12 \pm \sqrt{204}}{6}$$

steps.title: Match to the given form
steps.content: The quadratic formula gives two solutions: $\frac{12 + \sqrt{204}}{6}$ and $\frac{12 - \sqrt{204}}{6}$. One of these, $\frac{12 - \sqrt{204}}{6}$, has the form $\frac{12 - \sqrt{k}}{6}$, so $k = 204$.
